subject: Mathematics
title: Sample Spaces
description: A quick win: understand Sample Spaces.

hook.text: Before calculating odds, you need a list of everything that could possibly happen.

buildup.text: That list is the sample space. For a coin: {heads, tails}. For a die: {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6}.

discovery.text: Rolling two dice creates a sample space of 36 outcomes—not 12, because order matters.

twist.text: Many probability mistakes come from listing the sample space wrong or missing outcomes.

climax.text: Get the sample space right and the math almost does itself.

punchline.text: Probability starts by counting what's possible.

quiz.question: How many outcomes are in the sample space for rolling two dice?
